scope: Recent local-planner, social-navigation, and dynamic-obstacle repositories surveyed for Robot SF-native follow-up ideas.

included: Active or recently updated repositories with planner, local-risk, social-navigation, or benchmark relevance.
Repositories with public URLs and enough README or release metadata to identify transferable ideas.
excluded: Repositories that require code import before the idea can be evaluated.
Repositories whose only value is an upstream adapter without a clear Robot SF-native diagnostic or benchmark use.

repositories.name: tud-amr/mpc_planner
repositories.transferable_idea: Topology-aware MPC diagnostics for distinct route hypotheses around dynamic obstacles; chance-constrained handling of non-Gaussian obstacle uncertainty.
repositories.robot_sf_boundary: Use as design inspiration for diagnostics only; do not import ROS/C++ planner code.

repositories.name: Zhefan-Xu/Intent-MPC
repositories.transferable_idea: Intent-prediction-conditioned MPC for dynamic obstacle avoidance.
repositories.robot_sf_boundary: UAV framing is not directly portable; useful mainly for intent-conditioned risk interfaces.

repositories.name: TommasoVandermeer/social-jym
repositories.transferable_idea: JAX-vectorized social-navigation training environment with multiple social-navigation policies and human motion models.
repositories.robot_sf_boundary: Good performance and training-architecture reference; JAX stack is not a near-term Robot SF dependency.

repositories.name: sepsamavi/safe-interactive-crowdnav
repositories.transferable_idea: Safe interactive crowd navigation combining MPC-style control, DWA comparison, and CrowdSimPlus social-force pedestrians.
repositories.robot_sf_boundary: Use for planner-interface and scenario ideas; direct import would need dependency and benchmark-contract review.

repositories.name: HauserDong/HomoMPC
repositories.transferable_idea: Homotopy-aware distributed MPC framing for multi-agent navigation.
repositories.robot_sf_boundary: Supports the topology-diagnostic follow-up; multi-agent ROS stack is not directly portable.

repositories.name: CMU-TBD/SocNavBench
repositories.transferable_idea: Grounded social-navigation benchmark structure with recorded pedestrian datasets and social-navigation metrics.
repositories.robot_sf_boundary: Scenario and metric reference only; any dataset reuse needs separate provenance and redistribution review.

repositories.name: CognitiveAISystems/Dynamic-Neural-Potential-Field
repositories.transferable_idea: Learned differentiable local potential or risk surface injected into MPC.
repositories.robot_sf_boundary: Prototype a Robot SF-native risk-surface contract before any planner integration.

repositories.name: Arena-Rosnav/arena-rosnav
repositories.transferable_idea: Navigation benchmark and DRL training workflow organization for ROS navigation stacks.
repositories.robot_sf_boundary: Benchmark structure may be useful; README metadata alone is not enough to claim concrete planner behavior.

repositories.name: nkuhzx/ssDRL
repositories.transferable_idea: Social-stress reward or metric framing for crowd-comfort navigation.
repositories.robot_sf_boundary: Older code; use concept only after Robot SF metric contract review.

repositories.name: FK-75/social-navigation-robot
repositories.transferable_idea: Conservative, neutral, and open proxemic-profile comparison for social navigation.
repositories.robot_sf_boundary: No clear license in GitHub metadata; do not import code or assets.

repositories.name: SPALaboratory/SiT-Dataset
repositories.transferable_idea: Socially interactive pedestrian trajectory dataset and semantic-map references for scenario staging.
repositories.robot_sf_boundary: Audit download and redistribution terms before deriving fixtures.

repositories.name: rst-tu-dortmund/mpc_local_planner
repositories.transferable_idea: Classic ROS base_local_planner MPC plugin structure and minimum-time/quadratic receding-horizon examples.
repositories.robot_sf_boundary: GPL and older code make it reference-only; not a Robot SF dependency candidate.

opened_follow_ups.theme: SiT Dataset terms and staging audit
